title: cerastium viscosum var. consanguineum
intro: Cerastium viscosum var. consanguineum, also known as sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the carnation family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Cerastium viscosum var. consanguineum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
